Use the Page Object Model pattern to encapsulate page interactions into reusable classes with descriptive methods
Prefer Playwright's built-in auto-waiting locator methods (click, fill, check) over manual waitForSelector calls
Use test.step() to annotate logical groups of actions within a test for better trace and report readability
Write small, focused test functions that test one behavior or user flow each
Use test.describe() to logically group related tests and share beforeEach/afterEach hooks
Use Playwright fixtures (test.extend) for reusable setup/teardown logic instead of beforeAll/afterAll when possible
Use async/await consistently; every Playwright API call returns a Promise
Use expect with Playwright's web-first assertions (toBeVisible, toHaveText, toHaveURL) for auto-retrying checks
Leverage API requests via page.request or APIRequestContext for test setup/teardown (seeding data, cleaning up)
Use test.use() to configure per-test or per-describe options like viewport, locale, or storageState
Parametrize tests with arrays or test.describe loops for data-driven scenarios
